features.name: Implement Real PDF Export
features.description: Replace fake HTML-based PDF export with real server-side or client-side PDF generation
features.detailedDescription: Current PDF export is just an HTML file with .pdf extension. Users expect real PDF functionality. Need to implement Puppeteer for server-side generation or jsPDF for client-side generation.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user has completed a design in the canvas
acceptanceCriteria.when: the user clicks PDF export and selects format options
acceptanceCriteria.then: a real PDF file is generated with visual fidelity to the canvas design

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design with custom fonts, colors, and complex layouts
acceptanceCriteria.then: the exported PDF maintains all styling, fonts, and layout properties

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design with 50+ elements and multiple pages
acceptanceCriteria.then: the PDF is generated within 10 seconds and all elements are properly rendered

features.name: Complete ZIP Export Functionality
features.description: Enhance ZIP export to include CSS, assets, and documentation for production-ready exports
features.detailedDescription: Current ZIP export is missing CSS files and assets, making exported files not production-ready. Need to extract all CSS, organize assets properly, and include README documentation.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user has completed a design with custom styling and assets
acceptanceCriteria.then: the ZIP file contains HTML, CSS, assets folder, and README documentation

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design using Tailwind CSS and custom styles
acceptanceCriteria.then: all CSS rules are extracted and included in a separate stylesheet file

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design with images, icons, and custom fonts
acceptanceCriteria.then: all assets are organized in an assets folder with proper file structure

acceptanceCriteria.given: a ZIP export is generated
acceptanceCriteria.then: a README.md file explains the file structure and how to use the exported files

features.name: Implement LLM Streaming
features.description: Add streaming API support for AI design generation with incremental JSON parsing and live feedback
features.detailedDescription: Current 60-90 second wait without feedback creates poor UX. Need to implement streaming API with incremental JSON parsing, loading states, and progress indicators.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a user submits a design prompt
acceptanceCriteria.when: the AI starts generating the response
acceptanceCriteria.then: the user sees real-time progress updates and streaming content

acceptanceCriteria.given: streaming JSON response is received
acceptanceCriteria.then: the system parses and validates JSON progressively without waiting for completion

acceptanceCriteria.given: a long-running AI generation request
acceptanceCriteria.then: users see loading indicators, progress bars, or token count updates

acceptanceCriteria.given: a streaming connection fails during generation
acceptanceCriteria.then: the user receives helpful error message and can retry the request

features.name: Implement Chart Rendering
features.description: Integrate Recharts library to render chart nodes instead of empty divs
features.detailedDescription: Chart nodes currently render as empty divs, making them visually invisible. Need to integrate Recharts library for proper chart visualization.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design contains a bar chart node with data
acceptanceCriteria.then: the bar chart displays correctly with proper axes, labels, and data visualization

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design contains a line chart node
acceptanceCriteria.then: the line chart displays with smooth curves, data points, and proper styling

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design contains a pie chart node
acceptanceCriteria.then: the pie chart displays with proper segments, labels, and legend

acceptanceCriteria.given: a chart node with editable data
acceptanceCriteria.then: the chart updates immediately to reflect the new data

features.name: Add Pseudo-States Support
features.description: Extend DesignNode schema to support hover, focus, and active states for interactive elements
features.detailedDescription: Designs feel static and non-interactive without pseudo-states. Need to add hoverStyle, focusStyle, and activeStyle to the DesignNode schema and implement rendering logic.

acceptanceCriteria.given: an element has hoverStyle defined with different background color
acceptanceCriteria.then: the element background changes to the hover color smoothly

acceptanceCriteria.given: an input element has focusStyle with border highlight
acceptanceCriteria.then: the border changes color and shows the focus indicator

acceptanceCriteria.given: a button has activeStyle with pressed state styling
acceptanceCriteria.when: user clicks and holds the button
acceptanceCriteria.then: the button shows the pressed state styling

acceptanceCriteria.given: an element is selected
acceptanceCriteria.when: user opens PropsPanel and navigates to pseudo-states section
acceptanceCriteria.then: hover, focus, and active styles can be edited independently

features.name: Implement Responsive Breakpoints
features.description: Add responsive breakpoint support (sm, md, lg, xl) to DesignNode schema and renderer
features.detailedDescription: Designs don't adapt to screen sizes - only single viewport. Need to add styleVariants with breakpoint-specific styling and implement responsive rendering logic.

acceptanceCriteria.given: an element needs different styling for mobile and desktop
acceptanceCriteria.when: user defines styleVariants with sm and lg breakpoints
acceptanceCriteria.then: the element applies different styles based on viewport width

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design with responsive breakpoints defined
acceptanceCriteria.when: user switches viewport size or uses device preview
acceptanceCriteria.then: the design adapts layout and styling for the selected viewport

acceptanceCriteria.given: an element has styleVariants defined
acceptanceCriteria.when: user edits styles for specific breakpoint in PropsPanel
acceptanceCriteria.then: changes only affect that breakpoint, other breakpoints remain unchanged

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design with responsive variants is exported
acceptanceCriteria.then: responsive CSS media queries are properly generated

features.name: Wire Collaboration UI Integration
features.description: Integrate existing useCollaboration hook into ZDesignApp for real-time collaboration
features.detailedDescription: Socket.IO backend exists and collaboration hook is available, but UI integration is missing. Need to wire up collaboration features in the main app component.

acceptanceCriteria.given: multiple users are viewing the same project
acceptanceCriteria.then: active collaborators are shown with their cursors and presence indicators

acceptanceCriteria.given: two users are editing the same project
acceptanceCriteria.when: one user makes changes to the design
acceptanceCriteria.then: changes appear on the other user's screen within 500ms

acceptanceCriteria.given: two users edit the same element simultaneously
acceptanceCriteria.then: last-write-wins strategy is applied and both users see consistent state

acceptanceCriteria.given: multiple users are collaborating
acceptanceCriteria.when: users move their cursors over the canvas
acceptanceCriteria.then: each user sees other users' cursor positions with name labels

prdMetadata.requirement: O7: Collaboration Not Wired in UI

features.name: Refactor Chat API Route
features.description: Split 1,205-line chat API route into manageable modules for better maintainability
features.detailedDescription: Chat API route is too large (1,205 lines), creating maintenance nightmare and high cognitive load. Need to split into logical modules.

acceptanceCriteria.given: the chat API route is 1,205 lines
acceptanceCriteria.then: the route is split into modules under 300 lines each

acceptanceCriteria.given: existing chat functionality works
acceptanceCriteria.then: all existing features continue to work without breaking changes

acceptanceCriteria.given: the refactored chat modules
acceptanceCriteria.then: modules are logically organized with clear responsibilities

acceptanceCriteria.given: chat modules are created
acceptanceCriteria.then: each has clear documentation explaining its purpose and exports

prdMetadata.requirement: T5: Chat API Route Too Large

features.name: Add React Error Boundaries
features.description: Implement React error boundaries to prevent white screen crashes and provide graceful error handling
features.detailedDescription: Component crashes currently result in white screens. Need to add React error boundaries for graceful error handling and user-friendly error messages.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a component throws an error during rendering
acceptanceCriteria.then: error boundary catches it and shows fallback UI instead of white screen

acceptanceCriteria.given: an error is caught by error boundary
acceptanceCriteria.then: users see helpful error message with recovery suggestions

acceptanceCriteria.given: an error is caught by error boundary
acceptanceCriteria.then: error details are logged for debugging with component stack trace

acceptanceCriteria.given: a recoverable error occurs
acceptanceCriteria.when: user clicks retry button in error fallback
acceptanceCriteria.then: the component attempts to recover and re-render

features.name: Enable TypeScript Strict Mode
features.description: Remove typescript.ignoreBuildErrors and fix all type errors for better type safety
features.detailedDescription: TypeScript build errors are currently ignored, allowing type errors to slip through. Need to enable strict mode and fix all type errors.

acceptanceCriteria.given: typescript.ignoreBuildErrors is currently true
acceptanceCriteria.then: all type errors are caught during build process

acceptanceCriteria.given: type errors exist in the codebase
acceptanceCriteria.then: all type errors are fixed with proper typing

acceptanceCriteria.given: functions lack proper type annotations
acceptanceCriteria.then: all functions have proper parameter and return type annotations

acceptanceCriteria.given: strict mode is enabled
acceptanceCriteria.then: type checking prevents runtime type errors

features.name: Migrate to PostgreSQL Database
features.description: Migrate from SQLite to PostgreSQL for production-ready multi-user support
features.detailedDescription: SQLite doesn't scale for multi-user production. Need to migrate to PostgreSQL while maintaining data integrity and minimal downtime.

acceptanceCriteria.given: PostgreSQL is chosen for production
acceptanceCriteria.then: PostgreSQL instance is running with proper configuration

acceptanceCriteria.given: SQLite database contains existing projects
acceptanceCriteria.then: all data is transferred to PostgreSQL without data loss

acceptanceCriteria.given: migration to PostgreSQL is planned
acceptanceCriteria.then: DATABASE_URL points to PostgreSQL and schema is synchronized

acceptanceCriteria.given: PostgreSQL is in use
acceptanceCriteria.when: multiple users access the system simultaneously
acceptanceCriteria.then: database handles concurrent requests without performance degradation

features.name: Implement Multi-Provider AI Integration
features.description: Add support for OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google AI providers in addition to Z.ai
features.detailedDescription: Currently only Z.ai is active. Need to integrate other AI providers with SDK integration, API key management, and provider abstraction layer.

acceptanceCriteria.given: user wants to use OpenAI for design generation
acceptanceCriteria.when: user enters OpenAI API key in settings
acceptanceCriteria.then: OpenAI provider becomes available for design generation

acceptanceCriteria.given: multiple AI providers are configured
acceptanceCriteria.then: design generation uses the selected provider

acceptanceCriteria.given: different AI providers are configured
acceptanceCriteria.then: each provider shows appropriate configuration options

acceptanceCriteria.given: primary AI provider fails
acceptanceCriteria.then: system automatically falls back to secondary provider or templates

features.name: Implement Rate Limiting
features.description: Add rate limiting middleware to prevent API abuse and control costs
features.detailedDescription: No rate limiting on AI endpoints creates risk of API abuse and cost overruns. Need to implement rate limiter middleware.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Apply rate limits to AI endpoints
acceptanceCriteria.given: rate limiting is implemented
acceptanceCriteria.when: user makes requests to AI endpoints
acceptanceCriteria.then: requests are limited per user tier and time window

acceptanceCriteria.given: user approaches rate limit
acceptanceCriteria.then: user sees remaining request count and reset time

acceptanceCriteria.given: user exceeds rate limit
acceptanceCriteria.then: user receives 429 status with retry-after header

acceptanceCriteria.given: different user tiers exist
acceptanceCriteria.then: each tier has appropriate rate limits (free, pro, enterprise)

features.summary: Add rate limiting middleware to prevent API abuse and control costs

features.name: Implement Layers Panel
features.description: Add nested tree view component for navigating design hierarchy in PropsPanel
features.detailedDescription: Navigation in complex designs is difficult without layers panel. Need to add nested tree view showing design hierarchy with selection and editing capabilities.

acceptanceCriteria.scenario: Show design hierarchy in layers panel
acceptanceCriteria.given: a design with nested elements is loaded
acceptanceCriteria.then: complete element hierarchy is displayed as nested tree structure

acceptanceCriteria.given: layers panel shows element hierarchy
acceptanceCriteria.when: user clicks element name in tree
acceptanceCriteria.then: element is selected in canvas and highlighted

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design has deep nesting (10+ levels)
acceptanceCriteria.then: navigation remains smooth and responsive

acceptanceCriteria.given: element is selected in layers panel
acceptanceCriteria.then: context menu shows common actions (delete, duplicate, rename)

features.name: Implement Icon SVG Rendering
features.description: Add Lucide-React SVG rendering for icons based on content field
features.detailedDescription: Icons currently appear as text instead of graphics. Need to integrate Lucide-React for proper SVG icon rendering based on icon names.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a design contains icon nodes with icon names
acceptanceCriteria.then: icons appear as SVG graphics instead of text

acceptanceCriteria.given: user wants to use icons from Lucide set
acceptanceCriteria.then: corresponding Lucide icon is rendered as SVG

acceptanceCriteria.given: an icon has custom size, color, and styling
acceptanceCriteria.then: SVG respects all style properties (size, color, stroke, etc.)

acceptanceCriteria.given: design with icons is exported
acceptanceCriteria.then: icons are included as SVG elements or proper icon references
